title: cylindropuntia alcahes subsp. burrageana
intro: Cylindropuntia alcahes subsp. burrageana, also known as the Burrage Cholla, is a type of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its cylindrical stems and sharp spines. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Burrage Cholla include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and protect against infection, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. Herbal preparations of Burrage Cholla can be made by infusing the plant's stems in hot water or oil to create teas, salves, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues.
